pattern: The three orthographic shadow areas come from counting non-zero cells, summing row maxima, and summing column maxima of the grid.
hints: Think of each projection as a flattened shadow cast along one axis: top looks straight down, front looks from one side, side looks from the other.

The top shadow is simply which grid cells are occupied (non-zero). The front shadow along each row is as tall as the tallest tower in that row. The side shadow along each column is as tall as the tallest tower in that column.
Iterate the grid once: add 1 for each non-zero cell (top), the row maximum (front), and the column maximum (side). Column maxima need a single pass over all rows.

edge_inputs.description: single cell grid with one tower
2x2 with two zeros on the diagonal
all cells are zero — no top projection and front/side are all zeros
single cell grid with zero tower
row maxima and column maxima differ when towers are skewed
uniform height grid so top+front+side each cover full n

stress.description: random n×n grid with moderate heights
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    grid = [[rng.randrange(0, 51) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: full 50x50 grid exercises all three projection sums

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def projectionArea(self, grid):
        rows = len(grid)
        cols = len(grid[0]) if grid else 0
        top = sum(1 for r in grid for v in r if v > 0)
        front = sum(max(row) for row in grid)
        side = sum(max(grid[r][c] for r in range(rows)) for c in range(cols))
        return top + front + side
